steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says the honeycomb conjecture did something to mathematicians' efforts for nearly 2,000 years, ending only when Hales proved it in 1999. The blank must describe the relationship between an unproven conjecture and the people trying to prove it across nearly two millennia.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — defied: To defy means to resist or withstand. An unsolved conjecture that resisted all attempts for ~2,000 years defied those efforts. This fits precisely. ✓
  • Choice B — prefigured: To prefigure means to foreshadow or anticipate. A conjecture does not foreshadow mathematicians' efforts to solve it — that relationship is inverted and nonsensical here. ✗
  • Choice C — epitomized: To epitomize means to be a perfect example of. The conjecture did not embody or represent mathematicians' efforts — it obstructed them. ✗
  • Choice D — displaced: To displace means to move out of position or replace. The conjecture did not replace mathematicians' efforts; it resisted them. ✗
That matches Choice A — defied.
